commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=e219cc01ea7993ebf18e9a999951e87bc552abb7)
commands.review_summary:
Adds a Google setup-policy helper and tests that make Gemini CLI setup backend registration conditional on `OPENCLAW_ENABLE_GOOGLE_GEMINI_CLI_HARNESS`, plus a new Google provider rebuild boundary doc.

PR surface: Source +15, Tests +48, Docs +45. Total +108 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main resolves setup CLI backends through manifest ownership and setup-api registration, while this patch makes `google-gemini-cli` registration absent by default. I did not run a live onboarding flow in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review is needed for the new Google setup env/default policy, branch conflicts, and missing real behavior proof; there is no safe automated repair until maintainers choose the compatibility path.

target.title: feat(google): gate Gemini CLI setup harness
target.body:
## Summary

Starts the Google provider rebuild by separating the deprecated Gemini CLI harness setup path from the supported Google provider setup boundary.

- adds an explicit `OPENCLAW_ENABLE_GOOGLE_GEMINI_CLI_HARNESS` opt-in policy
- stops the lightweight Google setup entry from registering the Gemini CLI backend unless that flag is enabled
- adds tests for the opt-in policy and setup registration behavior
- documents the intended Google provider rebuild boundary and follow-up work

## Why

The current Google surface mixes official API-backed providers with a CLI-backed harness path. The Gemini CLI OAuth/project discovery path can depend on Cloud Code Assist internals such as `cloudcode-pa.googleapis.com`, `v1internal:loadCodeAssist`, and `v1internal:onboardUser`. Those should not be required by default Google setup.

This PR does not remove the Gemini CLI harness. It keeps it available for explicit opt-in while making the supported setup boundary cleaner.

## Follow-up scope

- Move Gemini CLI registration out of default onboarding and into an explicit harness choice.
- Add a first-class Gemini API provider implementation for the current official API surface.
- Keep Vertex AI separate from AI Studio / Gemini API key auth.
- Remove Cloud Code Assist project discovery from provider auth paths.
- Add regression tests proving normal Google setup never touches Cloud Code Assist internal endpoints.

## Test plan

Not run locally from this connector session.

Recommended targeted check:

```bash
pnpm vitest run extensions/google/gemini-cli-harness-policy.test.ts extensions/google/setup-api.test.ts
```
target.author: jason-allen-oneal
